name: Pasta Primavera Gregory
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chives, basil or other fresh herbs
1 Black olive, grated zest of 1 orange
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 4 quarts water to a boil; add 2 tablespoons salt and stir in fettuccine. Cook until tender but still firm; drain immediately. Transfer the pasta to a large bowl, add the olive oil and chopped onion; toss gently.
2. Let cool to room temperature.
3. Bring another 4 quarts water to a boil. Add 2 tablespoons salt and the peas. Cook for 1 minute, drain and immediately plunge peas into ice water. Let stand 10 minutes; drain and pat dry.
4. Add peas to pasta along with prosciutto, tomatoes, red peppers, scallions, and herbs to taste. Season with salt and pepper, sprinkle on raspberry vinegar to taste, and toss gently.
5. Toss the pasta primavera with Parmesan cheese. Arrange on serving platter. Scatter olives and citrus zest over the pasta and serve at room temperature.
